title: chore: decommission LTX and ACE-Step
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the `ltx-2` video model and `acestep` audio model from the shared registries because usage is low relative to maintenance cost.
- Deletes their runtime dispatch/handler code and model-specific environment configuration.
- Updates API docs, SDK guidance, generated bindings, and pricing snapshots.
- Preserves Sana routing, shared GPU authentication, Lambda access, and historical economics/observability data.

Operational note: merging/deploying this PR removes API exposure but does not stop host processes or lower the Lambda bill. Sana currently uses `ltx2-backend.pollinations.ai`; that hostname/tunnel must remain. Stop ACE/LTX only by their exact services/ports after deployment.

Tests:
- Enter registry/pricing snapshots: 264 passed
- Gen permissions, backend registry, and Sana fixed-backend regression: 32 passed
- Enter and Gen typechecks
- APIDOCS validation

Related: #11125

gist.headline: The model garden gets pruned
gist.blurb: LTX-2 and ACE-Step have left the shared model registry, while Sana’s route and the surrounding GPU plumbing stay carefully watered. Fewer low-traffic branches, same healthy garden.
gist.summary: Removed the ltx-2 video and acestep audio models from the public registries, API documentation, SDK types, pricing snapshots, and runtime dispatch while preserving Sana’s fixed LTX2 backend routing and shared GPU access.
gist.impact: API and SDK users now see a cleaner supported-model list without ltx-2 or acestep, while Sana continues using ltx2-backend.pollinations.ai unchanged.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy maintenance scene: Polly, wearing a tiny gardener’s hat and tool belt, carefully prunes two clearly labeled code-plants, “LTX-2” and “ACE-Step,” from a shared model garden. Nearby, a healthy Sana plant remains connected to a glowing backend tunnel, showing that its route stays intact.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener hat and tool belt, gently pruning two small code-plants labeled “LTX-2” and “ACE-Step” from a warm cream garden while a healthy “Sana” plant stays connected to a glowing retro server tunnel. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, flowers and vines, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, dark purple (#110518) pixel-font labels, nostalgic but beautiful.
